<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsukahanazuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tsukahanazuka Kofun | native_name =塚花塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukahanazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukahanazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukahanazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|44|N|130|47|55|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|44|N|130|47|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tsukahanazuka Kofun'''|塚花塚古墳}} is a [[kofun|Kofun]] from the [[Kofun period]], located in the Asada neighborhood of [[:en:Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191912|title=塚花塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukahanazuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumulus, with a diameter of approximately 30 meters and a height of about six meters. Situated on the left bank of the [[:en:Chikugo River|Chikugo River]] at the edge of an [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] that originates from the foot of Mount Minou. Tsukahanazuka Kofun is notable for its [[decorated kofun]] and unique burial structure. It contains a multi-room horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], although part of the corridor leading to the burial chamber has been lost. The remaining structure has a total length of eight meters, with the anterior chamber measuring two meters in length and 2.5 meters in width at its widest point, while the posterior chamber is 3.4 meters long and 2.5 meters at its maximum width.
The decorative elements of the kofun are centered on the back wall of the posterior chamber, extending to both adjacent walls. The designs include a red double concentric circle on the left wall, and both double and triple concentric circles in red and green on the right wall. The mural on the back wall is divided into three sections: the upper row features a long [[:en:Bracken|''warabi'' fern]] pattern with an unusual shape, the middle row contains two tiers of concentric circles, and additional warabi fern patterns adorn the surrounding walls. A small shield-shaped trapezoidal design and a series of triangular patterns appear at the bottom.
[[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered from this tumulus include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, copper saddle fittings, a large sword, and approximately 400 small red beads. These items are on display at the Ukiha City History and Folklore Museum. The tumulus is accessible by a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Ukiha Station|Ukiha Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyūdai Main Line|Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0037093/index.html Ukiha City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://ukihalove.jp/contents/medurashidukakofun/ Ukiha Trip Company] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsukahanazuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tsukahanazuka Kofun | native_name =塚花塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukahanazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukahanazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukahanazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|44|N|130|47|55|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|44|N|130|47|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tsukahanazuka Kofun'''|塚花塚古墳}} is a [[kofun|Kofun]] from the [[Kofun period]], located in the Asada neighborhood of [[:en:Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191912|title=塚花塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukahanazuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumulus, with a diameter of approximately 30 meters and a height of about six meters. Situated on the left bank of the [[:en:Chikugo River|Chikugo River]] at the edge of an [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] that originates from the foot of Mount Minou. Tsukahanazuka Kofun is notable for its [[decorated kofun]] and unique burial structure. It contains a multi-room horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], although part of the corridor leading to the burial chamber has been lost. The remaining structure has a total length of eight meters, with the anterior chamber measuring two meters in length and 2.5 meters in width at its widest point, while the posterior chamber is 3.4 meters long and 2.5 meters at its maximum width.
The decorative elements of the kofun are centered on the back wall of the posterior chamber, extending to both adjacent walls. The designs include a red double concentric circle on the left wall, and both double and triple concentric circles in red and green on the right wall. The mural on the back wall is divided into three sections: the upper row features a long [[:en:Bracken|''warabi'' fern]] pattern with an unusual shape, the middle row contains two tiers of concentric circles, and additional warabi fern patterns adorn the surrounding walls. A small shield-shaped trapezoidal design and a series of triangular patterns appear at the bottom.
[[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered from this tumulus include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, copper saddle fittings, a large sword, and approximately 400 small red beads. These items are on display at the Ukiha City History and Folklore Museum. The tumulus is accessible by a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Ukiha Station|Ukiha Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyūdai Main Line|Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0037093/index.html Ukiha City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://ukihalove.jp/contents/medurashidukakofun/ Ukiha Trip Company] {{in lang|ja}}

The decorative elements of the kofun are centered on the back wall of the posterior chamber, extending to both adjacent walls. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsukahanazuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tsukahanazuka Kofun | native_name =塚花塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukahanazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukahanazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukahanazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|44|N|130|47|55|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|44|N|130|47|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tsukahanazuka Kofun'''|塚花塚古墳}} is a [[kofun|Kofun]] from the [[Kofun period]], located in the Asada neighborhood of [[:en:Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191912|title=塚花塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukahanazuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumulus, with a diameter of approximately 30 meters and a height of about six meters. Situated on the left bank of the [[:en:Chikugo River|Chikugo River]] at the edge of an [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] that originates from the foot of Mount Minou. Tsukahanazuka Kofun is notable for its [[decorated kofun]] and unique burial structure. It contains a multi-room horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], although part of the corridor leading to the burial chamber has been lost. The remaining structure has a total length of eight meters, with the anterior chamber measuring two meters in length and 2.5 meters in width at its widest point, while the posterior chamber is 3.4 meters long and 2.5 meters at its maximum width.
The decorative elements of the kofun are centered on the back wall of the posterior chamber, extending to both adjacent walls. The designs include a red double concentric circle on the left wall, and both double and triple concentric circles in red and green on the right wall. The mural on the back wall is divided into three sections: the upper row features a long [[:en:Bracken|''warabi'' fern]] pattern with an unusual shape, the middle row contains two tiers of concentric circles, and additional warabi fern patterns adorn the surrounding walls. A small shield-shaped trapezoidal design and a series of triangular patterns appear at the bottom.
[[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered from this tumulus include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, copper saddle fittings, a large sword, and approximately 400 small red beads. These items are on display at the Ukiha City History and Folklore Museum. The tumulus is accessible by a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Ukiha Station|Ukiha Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyūdai Main Line|Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

[[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered from this tumulus include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, copper saddle fittings, a large sword, and approximately 400 small red beads. These items are on display at the Ukiha City History and Folklore Museum. The tumulus is accessible by a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Ukiha Station|Ukiha Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyūdai Main Line|Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The mural on the back wall is divided into three sections: the upper row features a long [[:en:Bracken|''warabi'' fern]] pattern with an unusual shape, the middle row contains two tiers of concentric circles, and additional warabi fern patterns adorn the surrounding walls. A small shield-shaped trapezoidal design and a series of triangular patterns appear at the bottom.
[[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered from this tumulus include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, copper saddle fittings, a large sword, and approximately 400 small red beads. These items are on display at the Ukiha City History and Folklore Museum. The tumulus is accessible by a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Ukiha Station|Ukiha Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyūdai Main Line|Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

It contains a multi-room horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], although part of the corridor leading to the burial chamber has been lost. The remaining structure has a total length of eight meters, with the anterior chamber measuring two meters in length and 2.5 meters in width at its widest point, while the posterior chamber is 3.4 meters long and 2.5 meters at its maximum width.
The decorative elements of the kofun are centered on the back wall of the posterior chamber, extending to both adjacent walls. The designs include a red double concentric circle on the left wall, and both double and triple concentric circles in red and green on the right wall. The mural on the back wall is divided into three sections: the upper row features a long [[:en:Bracken|''warabi'' fern]] pattern with an unusual shape, the middle row contains two tiers of concentric circles, and additional warabi fern patterns adorn the surrounding walls. A small shield-shaped trapezoidal design and a series of triangular patterns appear at the bottom.
[[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered from this tumulus include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, copper saddle fittings, a large sword, and approximately 400 small red beads. These items are on display at the Ukiha City History and Folklore Museum. The tumulus is accessible by a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Ukiha Station|Ukiha Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyūdai Main Line|Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

Situated on the left bank of the [[:en:Chikugo River|Chikugo River]] at the edge of an [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] that originates from the foot of Mount Minou. The remaining structure has a total length of eight meters, with the anterior chamber measuring two meters in length and 2.5 meters in width at its widest point, while the posterior chamber is 3.4 meters long and 2.5 meters at its maximum width.
The decorative elements of the kofun are centered on the back wall of the posterior chamber, extending to both adjacent walls. The mural on the back wall is divided into three sections: the upper row features a long [[:en:Bracken|''warabi'' fern]] pattern with an unusual shape, the middle row contains two tiers of concentric circles, and additional warabi fern patterns adorn the surrounding walls. A small shield-shaped trapezoidal design and a series of triangular patterns appear at the bottom.
[[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered from this tumulus include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, copper saddle fittings, a large sword, and approximately 400 small red beads. These items are on display at the Ukiha City History and Folklore Museum. The tumulus is accessible by a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Ukiha Station|Ukiha Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyūdai Main Line|Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsukahanazuka Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsukahanazuka Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsukahanazuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tsukahanazuka Kofun | native_name =塚花塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukahanazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukahanazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukahanazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|44|N|130|47|55|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|en|Ukiha, Fukuoka|WD=Q850373|lt=Ukiha, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|44|N|130|47|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tsukahanazuka Kofun'''|塚花塚古墳}} is a [[kofun|Kofun]] from the [[Kofun period]], located in the Asada neighborhood of {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|en|Ukiha, Fukuoka|WD=Q850373|lt=Ukiha}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191912|title=塚花塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukahanazuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumulus, with a diameter of approximately 30 meters and a height of about six meters. Situated on the left bank of the {{ill|Chikugo River|en|Chikugo River|WD=Q954320|lt=Chikugo River}} at the edge of an {{ill|Alluvial fan|en|Alluvial fan|WD=Q465088|lt=Alluvial fan}} that originates from the foot of Mount Minou. Tsukahanazuka Kofun is notable for its [[decorated kofun]] and unique burial structure. It contains a multi-room horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], although part of the corridor leading to the burial chamber has been lost. The remaining structure has a total length of eight meters, with the anterior chamber measuring two meters in length and 2.5 meters in width at its widest point, while the posterior chamber is 3.4 meters long and 2.5 meters at its maximum width.
The decorative elements of the kofun are centered on the back wall of the posterior chamber, extending to both adjacent walls. The designs include a red double concentric circle on the left wall, and both double and triple concentric circles in red and green on the right wall. The mural on the back wall is divided into three sections: the upper row features a long {{ill|Bracken|en|Bracken|WD=Q258034|lt=''warabi'' fern}} pattern with an unusual shape, the middle row contains two tiers of concentric circles, and additional warabi fern patterns adorn the surrounding walls. A small shield-shaped trapezoidal design and a series of triangular patterns appear at the bottom.
{{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} recovered from this tumulus include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery, copper saddle fittings, a large sword, and approximately 400 small red beads. These items are on display at the Ukiha City History and Folklore Museum. The tumulus is accessible by a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ukiha Station|en|Ukiha Station|WD=Q4898188|lt=Ukiha Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|en|Kyūdai Main Line|WD=Q870569|lt=Kyūdai Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0037093/index.html Ukiha City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://ukihalove.jp/contents/medurashidukakofun/ Ukiha Trip Company] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Tsukahanazuka Kofun'''|塚花塚古墳}} is a [[kofun|Kofun]] from the [[Kofun period]], located in the Asada neighborhood of {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|en|Ukiha, Fukuoka|WD=Q850373|lt=Ukiha|1=Ukiha, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191912|title=塚花塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukahanazuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumulus, with a diameter of approximately 30 meters and a height of about six meters. Situated on the left bank of the {{ill|Chikugo River|en|Chikugo River|WD=Q954320|lt=Chikugo River|1=Chikugo River}} at the edge of an {{ill|Alluvial fan|en|Alluvial fan|WD=Q465088|lt=Alluvial fan|1=Alluvial fan}} that originates from the foot of Mount Minou. Tsukahanazuka Kofun is notable for its [[decorated kofun]] and unique burial structure. It contains a multi-room horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], although part of the corridor leading to the burial chamber has been lost. The remaining structure has a total length of eight meters, with the anterior chamber measuring two meters in length and 2.5 meters in width at its widest point, while the posterior chamber is 3.4 meters long and 2.5 meters at its maximum width.
The decorative elements of the kofun are centered on the back wall of the posterior chamber, extending to both adjacent walls. The designs include a red double concentric circle on the left wall, and both double and triple concentric circles in red and green on the right wall. The mural on the back wall is divided into three sections: the upper row features a long {{ill|Bracken|en|Bracken|WD=Q258034|lt=''warabi'' fern|1=Bracken}} pattern with an unusual shape, the middle row contains two tiers of concentric circles, and additional warabi fern patterns adorn the surrounding walls. A small shield-shaped trapezoidal design and a series of triangular patterns appear at the bottom.
{{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} recovered from this tumulus include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery, copper saddle fittings, a large sword, and approximately 400 small red beads. These items are on display at the Ukiha City History and Folklore Museum. The tumulus is accessible by a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Ukiha Station|en|Ukiha Station|WD=Q4898188|lt=Ukiha Station|1=Ukiha Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|en|Kyūdai Main Line|WD=Q870569|lt=Kyūdai Main Line|1=Kyūdai Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
